title: The Boy Who Told the Truth
moral: Telling the truth takes courage, and it is always the right choice, even when it is hard.
moralZh: 说真话需要勇气，即使不容易，它也永远是正确的选择。

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Tom was a cheerful boy who lived in a cozy little village at the foot of a green hill. One sunny afternoon, he was playing ball in the living room, even though his mother had told him not to.

paragraphs.speaker: Tom
paragraphs.text: "I'll be extra careful!" Tom said to himself. "Nothing will go wrong!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But then — CRASH! The ball knocked over his mother's favorite vase. It fell to the floor and broke into pieces.

paragraphs.speaker: Tom
paragraphs.text: "Oh no!" Tom whispered, his heart beating fast. "Mom is going to be so angry!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Tom felt very nervous. He quickly swept the pieces under the sofa and sat down quietly. When his mother came home, she noticed something was different.

paragraphs.speaker: Mom
paragraphs.text: "Tom, where is my vase?" Mom asked, looking at the empty table. "It's not there anymore."

paragraphs.speaker: Tom
paragraphs.text: Tom swallowed hard. "I... I don't know, Mom. Maybe the cat knocked it over."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But Tom's face turned red. He could not look his mother in the eye. That night, Tom could not sleep at all. He kept thinking about the broken vase.

paragraphs.speaker: Tom
paragraphs.text: "I feel terrible," Tom said to his stuffed bear. "I lied to Mom. What should I do?"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The next morning, Tom took a deep breath and walked into the kitchen. His mother was making breakfast.

paragraphs.speaker: Tom
paragraphs.text: "Mom, I need to tell you something," Tom said in a small voice. "I broke your vase yesterday. I was playing ball inside, and it was an accident. I'm really sorry I lied to you."

paragraphs.speaker: Mom
paragraphs.text: Mom put down her cooking spoon and looked at Tom quietly. Then she smiled gently. "Tom, I am disappointed that you broke the vase and that you lied. But I am very proud of you for telling the truth now. That takes real courage."

paragraphs.speaker: Tom
paragraphs.text: "Can you forgive me?" Tom asked, his eyes full of tears.

paragraphs.speaker: Mom
paragraphs.text: "Of course I forgive you," Mom said, giving Tom a warm hug. "The vase was just a thing. But your honesty — that is something truly treasure-worthy."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: From that day on, Tom always told the truth. He discovered that being honest made him feel lighter and happier inside. And his mother trusted him more than ever before.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Because telling the truth might be hard sometimes, but it is always the bravest thing you can do.
